paper.title: RXR-Mediated Remodeling of Transcriptional and Chromatin Landscapes in APP Mouse Brain: Insights from Integrated Single-Cell RNA and ATAC Profiling

glossary.APP/PS1: Transgenic Alzheimer's disease mouse model (APPswe/PS1deltaE9) expressing mutant human amyloid precursor protein and presenilin 1
glossary.Bexarotene: Synthetic retinoid X receptor agonist used for pharmacological activation of RXR signaling
glossary.RXR: Retinoid X receptor; ligand-activated nuclear receptor that forms obligate heterodimers with partner receptors to regulate gene expression
glossary.RXRalpha: Retinoid X receptor alpha isoform; functions as obligate heterodimer partner for over 20 other nuclear receptors
glossary.LXR: Liver X receptor (NR1H3); nuclear receptor that heterodimerizes with RXR and regulates cholesterol and lipid metabolism
glossary.CRE: Cis-regulatory element; non-coding DNA region that regulates transcription of nearby genes
glossary.snATAC-seq: Single-nucleus assay for transposase-accessible chromatin with sequencing; measures genome-wide chromatin accessibility at single-cell resolution
glossary.chromVAR: Computational method for inferring transcription-factor-associated accessibility from single-cell ATAC-seq data
glossary.TOBIAS: Transcription factor Occupancy prediction By Investigation of ATAC-seq Signal; computational framework for genome-wide TF footprinting from ATAC-seq data
glossary.Spi1: ETS-family transcription factor (PU.1) and microglia master regulator
glossary.Apoe: Apolipoprotein E gene; RXR target gene central to brain lipid transport and Alzheimer's disease pathology
glossary.Apoc1: Apolipoprotein C1 gene located at the same genomic locus as Apoe
glossary.Fkbp5: FK506 binding protein 5; associated with glucocorticoid receptor signaling and steroid hormone receptor trafficking
glossary.Hes1: Hes family BHLH transcription factor 1; key regulator of cell differentiation and proliferation in neural stem cells
glossary.Sik1: Salt-inducible kinase 1; serine/threonine kinase involved in metabolic regulation
glossary.Nupr1: Nuclear Protein 1; stress-responsive transcription regulator involved in cell-cycle, apoptosis, and autophagy
glossary.Pdgfra: Platelet-derived growth factor receptor alpha; marker of oligodendrocyte precursor cells
glossary.Mertk: MER proto-oncogene tyrosine kinase; receptor involved in phagocytosis and cell survival
glossary.Olig2: Oligodendrocyte transcription factor 2; key regulator of oligodendrocyte differentiation
glossary.Nfatc2: Nuclear Factor of Activated T Cells 2; immune regulator that physically interacts with RXRalpha and RARalpha
glossary.Neurod2: Neurogenic differentiation factor 2; transcription factor involved in neuronal differentiation and identified as a regulatory hub in RXR-activated neuronal networks

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation remodels the chromatin and transcriptional landscape of the APP/PS1 mouse brain through cell-type-specific regulatory cascades, orchestrating gene networks involved in neuroinflammation, lipid metabolism, and synaptic signaling relevant to Alzheimer's disease.
nodes.supporting_quote: Our study demonstrates that RXR activation remodels the chromatin and transcriptional landscape of the APP/PS1 mouse brain through cell-type-specific regulatory cascades.

nodes.claim_text: Integrated single-cell chromatin and transcriptomic profiling identifies all major brain cell populations in APP/PS1 mouse brain with preserved cellular composition between treatment conditions, establishing a foundation for cell-type-resolved analysis of RXR signaling.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq was performed on brain tissue from 5-month-old APP/PS1 mice treated with Bexarotene (n=5) or Vehicle (n=5), generating 61,353 nuclear profiles. scRNA-seq from the same model (Bexarotene n=4, Vehicle n=4) yielded 37,634 cells after quality filtering.
nodes.analysis_type: unsupervised clustering and cell type annotation
nodes.analysis_detail: snATAC-seq nuclei were filtered by TSS enrichment >4 and 1000-100000 mapped fragments. Doublets were removed. Iterative LSI and clustering identified cell populations. Cell type identities were assigned via ArchR unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq. scRNA-seq cells were filtered (200-5000 unique features, <50000 total counts, <10% mitochondrial), clustered at resolution 0.25 on 10 PCs, and annotated using MAST marker genes.
nodes.supporting_quote: Taken together, these analyses establish a high-quality single-cell and single-nucleus atlas of the APP/PS1 mouse brain.

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation drives cell-type-specific remodeling of chromatin accessibility concentrated in microglia and endothelial cells, linking differential accessibility to functional gene expression programs via peak-gene correlations.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq chromatin accessibility profiles from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5) were integrated with scRNA-seq data (Bexarotene n=4, Vehicle n=4) via ArchR unconstrained integration.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility analysis with peak-to-gene linkage
nodes.analysis_detail: The ArchR project was converted to Signac format. Differentially accessible peaks between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups were identified per cell type using the FindMarkers function in Signac with logistic regression (adjusted p < 0.05). Peak-to-gene linkages were computed by correlating peak accessibility with gene expression within 100 kb of each TSS, using the union of peaks present in >2% of cells in at least one cell type. Functional annotation was performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: We conclude that RXR activation drives cell-type-specific remodeling of chromatin accessibility, nominates upstream TF regulators with matched motif-expression relationships, and links differential accessibility to gene expression via peak-gene correlations.

nodes.claim_text: Transcription factor footprinting reveals RXR-centered regulatory circuits with concordant changes in binding and expression, and a hierarchical cascade architecture propagating through multiple downstream regulators across brain cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq chromatin accessibility profiles from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5) were used for transcription factor footprinting analysis across non-neuronal cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: transcription factor footprinting and regulatory network construction
nodes.analysis_detail: Accessible peak regions per cell type were called using MACS2 within Signac. TOBIAS (0.17.0) was applied: Tn5 insertion bias corrected with ATACorrect, footprint scores computed with ScoreBigWig, bound motif sites estimated with BINDetect. TFs with differential binding scores in the top/bottom 5% or -log10(p) above the 95th percentile were considered significantly altered. TF-TF regulatory networks were constructed by linking bound TFBSs within promoter regions to their target TF genes using the CreateNetwork module.
nodes.supporting_quote: Integration with transcriptomics confirmed consistency between binding and expression for a subset of TFs, and network analysis revealed an RXR-initiated cascade that propagates through multiple downstream regulators.

nodes.claim_text: Neuronal transcription factor networks maintain structural robustness under RXR activation while displaying selective rewiring of specific regulatory cascades and shifts in hub transcription factor influence.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from neuronal nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: neuronal transcription factor regulatory network reconstruction and comparison
nodes.analysis_detail: TF regulatory networks were reconstructed from footprint-informed edges in neurons for both Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions, starting from differentially bound TFs identified by TOBIAS BINDetect. Network properties including number of TFs (~134), number of edges, density (~0.18-0.20), and global connectivity were computed and compared between conditions. Hub regulators were identified by out-degree centrality.
nodes.supporting_quote: Neuronal TF networks remain structurally robust under RXR activation but display localized rewiring, with selective enhancement of cascades (e.g., Atoh1 -> Neurod2) and shifts in hub influence.

nodes.claim_text: ChIP-seq independently validates RXR binding at differentially accessible chromatin regions, with overlapping peaks concentrated at proximal regulatory elements and enriched in neurodegenerative disease pathways.
nodes.experiment_detail: RXR ChIP-seq was performed on APP/PS1 mouse brain tissue (Bexarotene n=2, Vehicle n=2) and overlapped with snATAC-seq differentially accessible regions.
nodes.analysis_type: ChIP-seq peak calling and overlap analysis with snATAC-seq
nodes.analysis_detail: ChIP-seq reads were aligned to GRCm38 using Bowtie2 (v2.5.4). Low-quality reads (mapping score <2) removed. Peak calling performed with MACS2 at q-value 0.05. Overlaps between ChIP-seq peaks and differentially accessible ATAC-seq peaks were determined using Bedtools (v2.31.1) with 10 kb genomic windows. Overlapping peaks were annotated by Uropa for genomic feature distribution. Pathway enrichment of overlapping peak-associated genes was performed with Metascape.
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR ChIP-seq confirmed that a large fraction of differentially accessible regions identified by ATAC-seq correspond to direct RXR binding sites, many of them proximal to gene promoters.

nodes.claim_text: Integrative multi-omic analysis links RXR binding to candidate cis-regulatory elements with novel transcription factor targets showing cell-type-specific expression patterns, confirming RXR acts through both direct binding and co-regulatory partnerships.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq integration
nodes.experiment_detail: Overlapping peaks from snATAC-seq and RXR ChIP-seq were integrated with ENCODE regulatory signatures and scRNA-seq expression data from APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: multi-omic integration for cis-regulatory element annotation
nodes.analysis_detail: Overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peak sequences were scanned for RXRalpha heterodimer motifs using MEME Suite FIMO (v5.5.7) with default settings, matching to JASPAR RXRalpha (PPARgamma::RXRalpha) motif profiles. Peaks with RXR motifs were cross-referenced with ENCODE candidate CRE signatures (enhancer-like and promoter-like). Cell-type-specific expression patterns of identified TFs were validated from scRNA-seq differential expression data.
nodes.supporting_quote: These analyses confirm that RXR acts not only through direct DNA binding but also via co-regulatory partnerships with other TFs, extending its influence into developmental, immune, and stress-response networks.

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation remodels chromatin accessibility at loci governing inflammatory responses, lipid metabolism, and cell migration while decreasing accessibility at chromatin remodeling and G protein-coupled receptor signaling loci.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from microglial nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and gene ontology enrichment in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in microglia were identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Functional annotation of genes associated with differential peaks was performed using DAVID (v6.8) for GO enrichment. Peak-to-gene linkages connected accessibility changes to gene expression differences at representative loci.
nodes.supporting_quote: Microglia and endothelial cells demonstrated the highest number of differential peaks at a distance <100 kb (Supplementary Table S2).

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, RXR activation produces extensive chromatin remodeling affecting transcription, angiogenesis, and cell migration, with the strongest coupling between accessibility changes and transcriptional responses among all cell types.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from endothelial cell nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and gene ontology enrichment in endothelial cells
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells were identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Functional annotation performed using DAVID (v6.8). The overlap between genes with differential accessibility and differential expression was assessed and compared across cell types.
nodes.supporting_quote: This cell type demonstrated the strongest overlap between differential accessibility and transcriptional responses.

nodes.claim_text: In astrocytes, RXR activation predominantly decreases chromatin accessibility at loci involved in transcriptional regulation, chromatin remodeling, and brain development.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from astrocyte nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and gene ontology enrichment in astrocytes
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes were identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Functional annotation performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: Overall, gene ontology analysis of genes associated with differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes indicated enrichment of biological categories such as regulation of transcription (Nupr1), chromatin remodeling, brain development (Figure S2A).

nodes.claim_text: In oligodendrocytes, RXR activation modestly alters chromatin accessibility at loci related to receptor tyrosine kinase signaling and nervous system development.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from oligodendrocyte nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility and gene ontology enrichment in oligodendrocytes
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in oligodendrocytes were identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Functional annotation performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: Bexa treatment affected categories such as transmembrane receptor protein tyrosine kinase signaling (Pdgfra and Mertk) and nervous system development (Zeb1 [60]) (Figure S2B).

nodes.claim_text: Systematic analysis of transcription factor motif enrichment in chromatin accessibility data prioritizes candidate regulators of brain cell-type identity and reveals distinct cell-type-specific regulatory signatures.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from major brain cell types in APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: transcription factor motif enrichment and activity correlation
nodes.analysis_detail: chromVAR within ArchR computed per-cell TF motif enrichment Z-scores. Pearson correlation was calculated between motif enrichment Z-scores and ATAC-inferred gene expression (gene scores) using ArchR correlateMatrices function (FDR < 0.01 and correlation > 0.5). Pearson correlation was also performed between ATAC-inferred gene expression and RNA expression from scRNA-seq. TFs passing both correlation thresholds were retained as high-confidence candidates.
nodes.supporting_quote: Examples include cell-type-specific regulators such as Pax6 (astrocytes), Lef1 (endothelium), Neurod6 (neurons), Runx3 (macrophages), Mafb (microglia), and Nfe2l3 (oligodendrocytes) (Figure 2D-F, representative TFs with matched motif/activity profiles).

nodes.claim_text: Motif enrichment analysis of chromatin accessibility data identified 88 high-confidence candidate transcription factor regulators whose motif accessibility correlated with both chromatin-inferred and RNA-based gene expression across major brain cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq was performed on brain tissue from 5-month-old APP/PS1 mice (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5), generating 61,353 nuclear profiles. scRNA-seq from the same model (Bexarotene n=4, Vehicle n=4) yielded 37,634 cells.
nodes.analysis_type: transcription factor motif enrichment and correlation analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: chromVAR within ArchR computed per-cell TF motif enrichment Z-scores across major cell types. Pearson correlation was calculated between motif enrichment Z-scores and ATAC-inferred gene activity (gene scores) and between ATAC-inferred expression and RNA expression from scRNA-seq. TFs with Pearson correlation > 0.5 and FDR < 0.01 for both metrics were retained as high-confidence candidate regulators, yielding 88 TFs.
nodes.supporting_quote: We then prioritized regulators by correlating motif enrichment with ATAC-inferred gene activity (gene scores) and with RNA expression, identifying 88 high-confidence candidates (Pearson > 0.5, FDR < 0.01; Table S1, list of candidate TFs).

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation altered chromatin accessibility at thousands of genomic loci in APP/PS1 mouse brain, with approximately 1600 peaks becoming more accessible and approximately 1300 peaks becoming less accessible compared to Vehicle.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq chromatin accessibility profiles from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5) were integrated with scRNA-seq data (Bexarotene n=4, Vehicle n=4) via ArchR unconstrained integration.
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Feature linkages between ATAC peaks and gene expression were computed within 100 kb of each TSS. Differentially accessible peaks between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups across five major cell types were identified using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p-value < 0.05). Peaks were categorized as upregulated (n=1605), non-significant (n=14,170), or downregulated (n=1310).
nodes.supporting_quote: Most peaks showed no significant difference between RXR-activated and Vehicle groups across the five major cell types (n = 14,170; denoted "ns" in the heatmap), but 1605 peaks were more accessible with RXR activation and 1310 were enriched in Vehicle

nodes.claim_text: Differentially accessible chromatin peaks following RXR activation were concentrated in microglia and endothelial cell clusters, with fewer changes in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, and neurons.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq chromatin accessibility profiles from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5), 61,353 nuclei across major brain cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific differential accessibility quantification
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks were identified separately for each cell type between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). The number of significant differential peaks per cell type was compared to determine which cell types showed the strongest chromatin remodeling response.
nodes.supporting_quote: Differentially accessible peaks were concentrated in microglia and endothelial clusters and less in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes and neurons

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility at loci associated with cell migration, cell differentiation, inflammatory response, phosphorylation, and high-density lipoprotein remodeling, including genes such as Ccr1, Cx3cr1, and Apoe.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from microglial nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: gene ontology enrichment of upregulated chromatin peaks in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks with increased accessibility in Bexarotene-treated microglia (adjusted p < 0.05 by logistic regression) were identified. Genes linked to upregulated peaks were annotated using DAVID (v6.8) for GO biological process enrichment. Representative genes in enriched categories were identified through peak-to-gene linkage analysis.
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility affecting categories such as cell migration (Ccr1 [43]), cell differentiation (Dusp6 [44]), inflammatory response (Cx3cr1 [45]), phosphorylation (Bmp2k, Cbl [46]), HDL remodeling (Apoe, Lipg, Lipc, Lpl [47]) (Figure 3C, left).

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation decreased chromatin accessibility at loci associated with chromatin remodeling, cell differentiation including the microglia master regulator Spi1, and G protein-coupled receptor signaling.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from microglial nuclei/cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: gene ontology enrichment of downregulated chromatin peaks in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks with decreased accessibility in Bexarotene-treated microglia (adjusted p < 0.05 by logistic regression) were identified. Genes linked to downregulated peaks were annotated using DAVID (v6.8) for GO biological process enrichment.
nodes.supporting_quote: Bexarotene also decreased chromatin accessibility in gene ontology categories such as chromatin remodeling transcription (Nr3c1, Fkbp5, both genes are specific for microglia and macrophages), cell differentiation (Notch2, Spi1 [48] gene is microglia master regulator), and the phospholipase C-activating G protein-coupled receptor signaling pathway (P2ry12 [45], Lrp1 [49]) (Figure 3C, right).

nodes.claim_text: Chromatin accessibility at the Apoe/Apoc1 locus was significantly upregulated in microglia following RXR activation, with multiple promoter-proximal peaks showing higher accessibility, and Apoe mRNA expression was also significantly increased.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from microglia in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential accessibility at the Apoe/Apoc1 genomic locus was assessed in microglia between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac. Peaks linked to Apoe (log2FC = 1.66, adj-p = 0.028) and Apoc1 (log2FC = 1.13, adj-p = 6.3 x 10^-33) were identified as significantly upregulated. Apoe mRNA expression was assessed via scRNA-seq differential expression using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = 0.42, adj-p = 3.23 x 10^-6). Coverage plots visualized promoter-proximal peak accessibility.
nodes.supporting_quote: Peaks linked to Apoe/Apoc locus were significantly upregulated in microglia (Apoe log2FC = 1.66; adj-p = 0.028 and Apoc1 log2FC = 1.13, adj-p = 6.3 x 10^-33) with several promoter-proximal peaks showing higher accessibility under RXR activation (Figure 3B, locus-level coverage and linked peaks).

nodes.claim_text: Approximately 20-27% of transcription factors showed concordant changes in both chromatin binding occupancy and differential gene expression across brain cell types following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data and scRNA-seq expression data from APP/PS1 mouse brain cell types (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: concordance analysis between transcription factor binding and expression
nodes.analysis_detail: For each cell type, TFs with significantly altered footprinting scores (TOBIAS BINDetect, top/bottom 5% or -log10(p) above 95th percentile) were compared to TFs with significant differential expression (MAST, Bonferroni-corrected p < 0.05). The percentage of TFs showing concordant changes (same direction in both footprinting and expression) was calculated per cell type.
nodes.supporting_quote: Comparing footprinting changes with differential expression (Figure 4), 20-27% of TFs showed consistent regulation at both levels (Figure 4C, Table S3).

nodes.claim_text: Transcription factor regulatory network analysis predicted a cascade of 25 primary transcription factors activated by ligand-bound RXR that collectively activate 141 additional transcription factors, revealing a hierarchical regulatory architecture across brain cell types.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from APP/PS1 mouse brain cell types (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: transcription factor regulatory network reconstruction
nodes.analysis_detail: The TOBIAS CreateNetwork module was used to construct TF-TF regulatory networks by linking bound TFBSs within promoter regions of TF-encoding genes. Starting from ligand-activated RXR, the cascade of primary TFs (directly activated by RXR) and secondary TFs (activated by primary TFs) was enumerated across brain cell types, identifying 25 primary and 141 secondary TFs.
nodes.supporting_quote: This analysis predicted a cascade initiated by ligand-activated RXR, involving 25 primary TFs that collectively activate 141 additional TFs, revealing an RXR-centered regulatory hierarchy across brain cell types.

nodes.claim_text: Neuronal transcription factor regulatory networks remained structurally robust under RXR activation, encompassing approximately 134 transcription factors with comparable network size, density, and global connectivity between RXR-activated and Vehicle conditions.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from neuronal nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: neuronal transcription factor network comparison between conditions
nodes.analysis_detail: TF regulatory networks were reconstructed from footprint-informed edges in neurons for both Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions, starting from differentially bound TFs identified by TOBIAS BINDetect. Network properties including number of TFs (~134), number of edges (thousands), density (~0.18-0.20), and global connectivity were computed and compared between conditions.
nodes.supporting_quote: The resulting networks encompassed 134 TFs and thousands of edges, with comparable size, density (~0.18-0.20), and global connectivity between conditions.

nodes.claim_text: A consistent core of universal neuronal hub regulators including Klf4, Egr2, Zfx, Foxn1, and Nrf1 maintained the highest network connectivity regardless of RXR activation status, forming a stable regulatory subnetwork.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from neuronal nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains.
nodes.analysis_type: hub transcription factor identification in neuronal networks
nodes.analysis_detail: In both Bexarotene and Vehicle neuronal TF networks, hub regulators were identified by out-degree centrality. TFs with the highest out-degrees across both conditions were classified as universal regulators. The stability of these hubs was assessed by comparing their rankings between conditions.
nodes.supporting_quote: Across both conditions, a consistent core of universal regulators--including Klf4, Egr2, Zfx, Foxn1, and Nrf1--emerged with the highest out-degrees, forming a stable, radiating subgraph irrespective of the initiating seed TF.

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation selectively enhanced specific transcription factor cascades in neurons, including the Atoh1-to-Neurod2 regulatory pathway, with Neurod2 emerging as a prominent regulatory hub in the RXR-activated network.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from neuronal nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: condition-specific cascade and hub analysis in neuronal networks
nodes.analysis_detail: Condition-specific regulatory cascades were identified by comparing TF-TF edge weights and hub connectivity between Bexarotene and Vehicle neuronal networks. The Atoh1-to-Neurod2 cascade was identified as selectively enhanced under RXR activation. Neurod2 hub status was assessed by its out-degree in the RXR-activated network compared to Vehicle.
nodes.supporting_quote: RXR activation selectively altered specific cascades and hubs. For example, a cascade from Atoh1 to Neurod2 was enhanced, and Neurod2 itself emerged as a prominent regulatory hub.

nodes.claim_text: Of approximately 12000 RXR binding peaks identified by ChIP-seq, approximately 3900 overlapped with differentially accessible chromatin regions, and among approximately 1250 shared peaks, roughly 70% were located at or near transcription start sites, indicating RXR binding is concentrated at proximal cis-regulatory elements.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from APP/PS1 mouse brains and RXR ChIP-seq from the same model (Bexarotene n=2, Vehicle n=2).
nodes.analysis_type: overlap analysis between ATAC-seq and ChIP-seq peaks with genomic feature annotation
nodes.analysis_detail: Overlaps between 11,957 ChIP-seq RXR-binding peaks and differentially accessible ATAC-seq peaks were determined using Bedtools (v2.31.1) with 10 kb genomic windows. Among 3,908 total overlapping ATAC-seq peaks, 1,251 shared peaks were further characterized. Genomic feature distribution was annotated using Uropa: peaks classified as TSS (777), upstream promoter (60), intronic, or intergenic.
nodes.supporting_quote: Among the 1251 overlapping peaks, 777 were located at transcription start sites (TSSs) and another 60 within upstream promoter regions, indicating that nearly 70% of overlapping peaks represent proximal cis-regulatory elements (CREs) likely contributing to gene regulation under RXR activation (Figure 5C).

nodes.claim_text: Genes near overlapping chromatin accessibility and RXR binding peaks were enriched in pathways associated with multiple neurodegenerative disorders including Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, Huntington's disease, prion disease, and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
nodes.experiment_detail: Overlapping peaks from snATAC-seq and RXR ChIP-seq in APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: pathway enrichment analysis of genes near overlapping peaks
nodes.analysis_detail: Genes associated with the 1,251 overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks were analyzed for pathway enrichment using Metascape for multilist comparative analysis against GO processes, KEGG pathways, Reactome gene sets, canonical pathways, and CORUM complexes.
nodes.supporting_quote: Many of the predicted target genes clustered in KEGG pathways associated with neurodegenerative disorders, including Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), Huntington's disease (HD), prion disease (PrD), and amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS).

nodes.claim_text: Integrative analysis of overlapping chromatin accessibility and RXR binding peaks within candidate regulatory element signatures identified seven transcription factors of particular interest - Myc, Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2, and Nfatc2 - each showing cell-type-specific expression patterns and regulatory associations.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq integration
nodes.experiment_detail: Overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks were cross-referenced with ENCODE regulatory signatures and scRNA-seq expression data from APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: candidate cis-regulatory element and transcription factor identification
nodes.analysis_detail: Overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peak sequences were scanned for RXRalpha heterodimer motifs using MEME Suite FIMO (v5.5.7) with default settings, matching to JASPAR RXRalpha (PPARgamma::RXRalpha) motif profiles. Peaks with RXR motifs were cross-referenced with ENCODE candidate CRE signatures (enhancer-like and promoter-like). Seven TFs with motifs within overlapping peaks matching ENCODE CRE annotations were identified. Cell-type-specific expression patterns validated from scRNA-seq.
nodes.supporting_quote: This approach identified seven TFs of particular interest--Myc, Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2, and Nfatc2--each showing cell-type-specific expression and regulatory associations (Figure 5G, lower panel).
nodes.dataset_link: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905: direct

nodes.claim_text: Among the seven transcription factors identified at overlapping chromatin and RXR binding sites, only Myc and Nfatc2 had previously recognized direct regulatory input from retinoid receptors, while Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, and Gli2 represent newly identified candidate RXR regulatory targets.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq integration
nodes.experiment_detail: Multi-omic integration of ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and scRNA-seq in APP/PS1 mouse brain, compared to published literature on known RXR targets.
nodes.analysis_type: literature comparison of identified transcription factor targets
nodes.analysis_detail: The seven TFs identified at overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks with CRE annotations were compared against published literature on known direct RXR regulatory targets. Two TFs (Myc and Nfatc2) had documented RXR/RAR heterodimer regulation; the remaining five (Arid5a, Creb3l2, Egr1, Zic2, Gli2) were classified as newly identified candidate RXR targets.
nodes.supporting_quote: Among the seven, only Myc and Nfatc2 showed previously recognized direct regulatory input from retinoid receptors, specifically RXR/RAR heterodimers.
nodes.dataset_link: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905: direct

nodes.claim_text: Differential expression analysis of neurodegeneration-related genes located near overlapping chromatin accessibility and RXR binding peaks confirmed altered expression across astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, and microglia, consistent with binding and accessibility predictions.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq integration
nodes.experiment_detail: scRNA-seq differential expression analysis of genes near overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks in APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: differential expression validation of neurodegeneration-associated genes
nodes.analysis_detail: Genes near overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks clustering in neurodegenerative disease KEGG pathways were assessed for differential expression between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using scRNA-seq. Differential expression tested separately in astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, and microglia using MAST with Bonferroni correction. Expression changes compared to footprinting predictions for consistency.
nodes.supporting_quote: Differential expression analysis of these neurodegeneration-related genes across astrocytes, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, and microglia confirmed altered expression consistent with the footprinting predictions (Figure 5G).
nodes.dataset_link: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905: direct

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation operates through a three-tiered regulatory architecture: direct RXR binding at accessible chromatin validated by ChIP-seq, secondary activation of partner transcription factors such as Nfatc2, Creb3l2, and Myc, and tertiary propagation through broader metabolic, inflammatory, and developmental gene expression networks.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq, ChIP-seq, and single-cell RNA-seq integration
nodes.experiment_detail: Integrative analysis of snATAC-seq (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5), RXR ChIP-seq (Bexarotene n=2, Vehicle n=2), and scRNA-seq (Bexarotene n=4, Vehicle n=4) from 5-month-old APP/PS1 mouse brain.
nodes.analysis_type: integrative multi-omic synthesis
nodes.analysis_detail: Findings from differential accessibility (snATAC-seq), TF footprinting (TOBIAS), ChIP-seq peak overlap, and differential expression (scRNA-seq) were synthesized. The first tier was defined by overlapping ChIP-seq/ATAC-seq peaks (direct RXR binding). The second tier was identified from TFs showing altered footprinting and expression as RXR network targets (e.g., Nfatc2, Creb3l2, Myc). The third tier was inferred from broader gene expression programs propagated by second-tier TFs.
nodes.supporting_quote: Our integrative analyses also show how RXR operates through (a) direct DNA binding/primary effects, corresponding to direct RXR binding at accessible chromatin regions validated by ChIP-seq; (b) through partnerships or secondary effects, reflected in RXR-dependent activation of TFs such as Nfatc2, Creb3l2, and Myc that themselves exhibit altered footprinting and expression; and finally (c) tertiary downstream remodeling
nodes.dataset_link: GEO: GSE283906; GEO: GSE289039; GEO: GSE283905: outside inventory

nodes.claim_text: Integrated single-cell chromatin and transcriptomic profiling of 5-month-old APP/PS1 mouse brain identified eight major cell populations: astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells, and choroid plexus epithelial/ependymal cells.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq (61,353 nuclei) and scRNA-seq (37,634 cells) from 5-month-old APP/PS1 mouse brains treated with Bexarotene or Vehicle.
nodes.analysis_type: unsupervised clustering and cell type annotation
nodes.analysis_detail: snATAC-seq nuclei processed through ArchR with iterative LSI and clustering. Cell identities assigned via unconstrained integration with scRNA-seq. scRNA-seq cells clustered using Seurat at resolution 0.25 on 10 PCs. Marker genes identified using MAST. Eight major populations annotated: astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/VSMCs, and choroid plexus epithelial/ependymal cells.
nodes.supporting_quote: Unsupervised clustering and UMAP projection identified major brain cell populations, including astrocytes, microglia, neurons, oligodendrocytes, endothelial cells, macrophages, pericytes/vascular smooth muscle cells, and a cluster containing choroid plexus epithelial and ependymal cells (Figure 1B, UMAP plots of integrated datasets).

nodes.claim_text: Global cellular composition was preserved between RXR-activated and Vehicle-treated groups in APP/PS1 mouse brain, with balanced lineage representation across conditions.
nodes.experiment_detail: Integrated snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: cell composition comparison between treatment groups
nodes.analysis_detail: UMAP projections of integrated snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data were assessed for overlap between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups. Proportions of annotated peaks across cell types were quantified per treatment group (n=5 per group) to confirm balanced lineage representation.
nodes.supporting_quote: Cells from RXR-activated and Vehicle groups mapped to highly overlapping clusters, indicating that global cellular composition was preserved across conditions.

nodes.claim_text: Chromatin accessibility patterns at cell-type marker gene loci paralleled transcriptomic expression patterns, and linkage analysis identified correlated regions of chromatin accessibility and gene activity across distinct cell populations.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq chromatin accessibility and scRNA-seq expression data from APP/PS1 mouse brain cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: peak-to-gene linkage and chromatin-expression correlation
nodes.analysis_detail: Chromatin accessibility at cell-type marker gene loci was compared to RNA expression patterns across cell types. Peak-to-gene linkage analysis performed using ArchR addPeak2GeneLinks function to identify significant correlations between peak accessibility and gene activity across distinct cell populations.
nodes.supporting_quote: Chromatin accessibility analysis of cell type-specific marker genes paralleled expression patterns (Figure 1E, accessibility tracks for representative marker genes), further supporting robust annotation. Peak-to-gene linkage analysis highlighted correlated regions of chromatin accessibility and gene activity across distinct cell populations (Figure 1E, bottom), providing an initial framework for mapping RXR-dependent cis-regulatory interactions.

nodes.claim_text: Candidate transcription factor regulators showed cell-type specificity, with Pax6 enriched in astrocytes, Lef1 in endothelial cells, Neurod6 in neurons, Runx3 in macrophages, Mafb in microglia, and Nfe2l3 in oligodendrocytes.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq motif enrichment and scRNA-seq expression data from APP/PS1 mouse brain cell types.
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type-specific transcription factor activity profiling
nodes.analysis_detail: From the 88 high-confidence candidate TF regulators, cell-type specificity was assessed by comparing chromVAR motif enrichment Z-scores, ATAC-inferred gene activity, and RNA expression levels across astrocytes, endothelial cells, neurons, macrophages, microglia, and oligodendrocytes. TFs with enrichment concentrated in a single cell type were classified as cell-type-specific regulators.
nodes.supporting_quote: Examples include cell-type-specific regulators such as Pax6 (astrocytes), Lef1 (endothelium), Neurod6 (neurons), Runx3 (macrophages), Mafb (microglia), and Nfe2l3 (oligodendrocytes)

nodes.claim_text: In microglia, RXR activation produced approximately 1750 differentially accessible chromatin peaks, with roughly equal numbers showing increased (~856) and decreased (~816) accessibility.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from microglial nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility quantification in microglia
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in microglia were identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Peaks classified as upregulated (856) or downregulated (816). Total: 1752 differential peaks.
nodes.supporting_quote: In microglia, there were 1752 differential peaks (856 upregulated and 816 downregulated).

nodes.claim_text: Chromatin peaks linked to the Fkbp5 locus were significantly downregulated in microglia following RXR activation, coinciding with decreased Fkbp5 mRNA expression.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from microglia in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential accessibility at the Fkbp5 locus assessed in microglia using logistic regression in Signac. Two peaks linked to Fkbp5 were significantly downregulated (log2FC = -0.94, adj-p = 2.2 x 10^-5). Fkbp5 mRNA assessed via scRNA-seq using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = -0.16, adj-p = 6.6 x 10^-6).
nodes.supporting_quote: We identified two peaks linked to Fkbp5 locus which were significantly downregulated in microglia (log2FC = -0.94; adj-p = 2.2 x 10^-5), coinciding with mRNA downregulation (log2FC = -0.16; adj-p = 6.6 x 10^-6) in these cells

nodes.claim_text: Endothelial cells showed approximately 1700 differentially accessible chromatin peaks following RXR activation, and this cell type demonstrated the strongest overlap between differential accessibility and transcriptional responses among all cell types examined.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from endothelial cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility quantification and transcriptional overlap in endothelial cells
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Upregulated: 1717, downregulated: 928. Overlap between genes with differential accessibility and differential expression compared across cell types.
nodes.supporting_quote: Endothelial cells showed 1717 differential peaks increasing chromatin accessibility and 928 decreasing it (Supplementary Table S2). This cell type demonstrated the strongest overlap between differential accessibility and transcriptional responses.

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, RXR activation upregulated chromatin accessibility and expression at loci involved in transcriptional regulation, angiogenesis, cell migration, and cytoskeletal organization, including genes such as Hes1, Bach2, Id3, and Bcam.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from endothelial cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: gene ontology enrichment of upregulated chromatin peaks in endothelial cells
nodes.analysis_detail: Genes linked to upregulated differentially accessible peaks in endothelial cells (adjusted p < 0.05) were annotated using DAVID (v6.8) for GO biological process enrichment.
nodes.supporting_quote: Upregulated GO categories were regulation of transcription involved in different cell functions (Hes1, Bach2, Id3) (Figure 3D, left).

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, the Hes1 locus showed significantly increased chromatin accessibility with concordant mRNA upregulation following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from endothelial cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential accessibility at the Hes1 locus assessed in endothelial cells using logistic regression in Signac (log2FC = 0.70, adj-p = 2.73 x 10^-8). Hes1 mRNA assessed via scRNA-seq using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = 0.38, adj-p = 6.9 x 10^-29).
nodes.supporting_quote: Figure 3B shows the differentially accessible peaks and the representative locus of Hes1 gene in endothelial cells (log2FC = 0.70; adj-p = 2.73 x 10^-8) and its mRNA upregulation (log2FC = 0.38; adj-p = 6.9 x 10^-29).

nodes.claim_text: In endothelial cells, the Sik1 locus showed decreased chromatin accessibility with concordant mRNA downregulation following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from endothelial cells in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential accessibility at the Sik1 locus assessed in endothelial cells using logistic regression in Signac (log2FC = -0.29, adj-p = 3.55 x 10^-8). Sik1 mRNA assessed via scRNA-seq using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = -0.14, adj-p = 1 x 10^-4).
nodes.supporting_quote: Figure 3B shows the differentially accessible peak of Sik1 locus in endothelial cells (log2FC = -0.29; adj-p = 3.55 x 10^-8) and its mRNA downregulation (log2FC = -0.14; adj-p = 1 x 10^-4).

nodes.claim_text: In astrocytes, RXR activation produced approximately 276 differentially accessible peaks, predominantly showing decreased accessibility (~252 downregulated vs ~26 upregulated), with enrichment in biological categories including transcriptional regulation, chromatin remodeling, and brain development.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from astrocyte nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility quantification and gene ontology enrichment in astrocytes
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in astrocytes identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Upregulated: 26, downregulated: 252. GO enrichment performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: In astrocytes, there were 276 differential peaks (26 up- and 252 downregulating, Supplementary Table S2).

nodes.claim_text: In astrocytes, RXR activation increased chromatin accessibility near the Nupr1 transcription start site, accompanied by increased Nupr1 mRNA expression.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from astrocytes in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential accessibility near Nupr1 TSS assessed in astrocytes using logistic regression in Signac (log2FC = 0.52, adj-p = 6.28 x 10^-4). Nupr1 mRNA assessed via scRNA-seq using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = 0.14, adj-p = 2.2 x 10^-9).
nodes.supporting_quote: Treatment increased chromatin accessibility near the Nupr1 transcription start site (log2FC = 0.52; adj-p = 6.28 x 10^-4) that was associated with increased expression (mRNA log2FC = 0.14; adj-p = 2.2 x 10^-9).

nodes.claim_text: In oligodendrocytes, RXR activation produced approximately 185 differentially accessible peaks (~64 upregulated and ~121 downregulated), affecting biological categories related to receptor tyrosine kinase signaling and nervous system development.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from oligodendrocyte nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility quantification and gene ontology enrichment in oligodendrocytes
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in oligodendrocytes identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Upregulated: 64, downregulated: 121. GO enrichment performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: In Oligodendrocytes there were 185 differential peaks (64 up- and 121 downregulating, Supplementary Table S2).

nodes.claim_text: In oligodendrocytes, Pdgfra showed increased mRNA expression with concordant increased chromatin accessibility at its promoter following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from oligodendrocytes in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Pdgfra mRNA assessed in oligodendrocytes using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = 0.27, adj-p = 0.021). Chromatin accessibility at promoter-proximal peak assessed using logistic regression in Signac (log2FC = 0.72, adj-p = 4.9 x 10^-4).
nodes.supporting_quote: Pdgfra is an oligodendrocytes specific gene which was upregulated after treatment (mRNA log2FC = 0.27; adj-p = 0.021), matching the increased chromatin accessibility with a peak close to the promoter (log2FC = 0.72; adj-p = 4.9 x 10^-4, see Figure 3B for coverage plots of differentially accessible peaks).

nodes.claim_text: In oligodendrocytes, Mertk showed increased mRNA expression accompanied by increased chromatin accessibility at a promoter-proximal peak following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and scRNA-seq data from oligodendrocytes in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: locus-level differential accessibility and expression analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: Mertk mRNA assessed in oligodendrocytes using MAST with Bonferroni correction (log2FC = 0.11, adj-p = 0.03). Chromatin accessibility at promoter-proximal peak assessed using logistic regression in Signac (log2FC = 0.51, adj-p = 0.006).
nodes.supporting_quote: Mertk, a tyrosine kinase that was reported upregulated by Bexarotene [62]) also showed increased expression after treatment in our study (mRNA log2FC = 0.11; adj-p = 0.03), accompanied by the increased chromatin accessibility with a peak close to the promoter (log2FC = 0.51; adj-p = 0.006).

nodes.claim_text: Neurons showed relatively few differentially accessible peaks (~110 total) following RXR activation, with affected loci associated with dendrite morphogenesis, synaptic transmission, and regulation of synaptic plasticity.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq from neuronal nuclei in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene n=5, Vehicle n=5).
nodes.analysis_type: differential accessibility quantification and gene ontology enrichment in neurons
nodes.analysis_detail: Differentially accessible peaks in neurons identified between Bexarotene and Vehicle using logistic regression in Signac (adjusted p < 0.05). Total: 110 differential peaks. GO enrichment performed using DAVID (v6.8).
nodes.supporting_quote: There were relatively small number of differential peaks in Neurons (total number 110, see Supplementary Table S2). The GO terms were associated with dendrite morphogenesis, synaptic transmission, and regulation of synaptic plasticity (Supplementary Table S2).

nodes.claim_text: Several transcription factors including Ets1, Erg, and Gabpa showed stronger chromatin binding in the Vehicle-treated group across all four non-neuronal cell types examined (astrocytes, endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes).
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from non-neuronal cell types in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential transcription factor binding analysis across cell types
nodes.analysis_detail: TOBIAS BINDetect identified TFs with significantly different binding scores between Bexarotene and Vehicle in each non-neuronal cell type. TFs with scores in the top/bottom 5% or -log10(p) above 95th percentile considered significant. TFs showing consistent higher binding in Vehicle across all four cell types identified.
nodes.supporting_quote: Some TFs--including Ets1, Erg, and Gabpa--showed stronger binding in the Vehicle group across all four cell types

nodes.claim_text: Neurod2 exhibited higher chromatin binding in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from non-neuronal cell types in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: differential transcription factor binding analysis
nodes.analysis_detail: TOBIAS BINDetect compared Neurod2 binding scores between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes. Neurod2 identified as showing higher binding in the Bexarotene group in these three cell types.
nodes.supporting_quote: whereas Neurod2 exhibited higher binding in endothelial cells, microglia, and oligodendrocytes.

nodes.claim_text: Transcription factors with altered binding in astrocytes were primarily unique to that cell type, and astrocytes displayed the most prominent cell-type-specific responses to RXR activation among the non-neuronal cell types examined.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from astrocytes and other cell types in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: cell-type specificity analysis of differentially bound transcription factors
nodes.analysis_detail: TFs with significantly altered binding in astrocytes (TOBIAS BINDetect, top/bottom 5% or -log10(p) above 95th percentile) were cross-referenced with TFs altered in other cell types. Degree of overlap assessed to determine cell-type specificity.
nodes.supporting_quote: By contrast, TFs identified in astrocytes were primarily unique to these cells and displayed the most prominent cell type-specific responses.

nodes.claim_text: Olig2 showed concordant increases in both chromatin binding occupancy and gene expression in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes following RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_type: single-nucleus ATAC-seq integrated with single-cell RNA-seq
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting and scRNA-seq expression data from astrocytes and oligodendrocytes in APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: concordance analysis of transcription factor binding and expression
nodes.analysis_detail: Olig2 footprinting scores compared between Bexarotene and Vehicle in astrocytes (log2FC = 0.02) and oligodendrocytes (log2FC = 0.01) using TOBIAS BINDetect. Olig2 differential expression assessed using MAST with Bonferroni correction in astrocytes (log2FC = 0.09) and oligodendrocytes (log2FC = 0.13).
nodes.supporting_quote: For example, Olig2 had higher footprinting scores in astrocytes and oligodendrocytes (astro-foot log2FC = 0.02, oligo-foot log2FC = 0.01) and also showed higher gene expression (astro-DEG log2FC = 0.09, oligo-DEG log2FC = 0.13).

nodes.claim_text: In the Vehicle-treated neuronal transcription factor network, Foxn1 and RXRalpha served as broad cascade initiators and key integrator hubs.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq footprinting data from neuronal nuclei in Vehicle-treated APP/PS1 mouse brains.
nodes.analysis_type: hub identification in Vehicle neuronal transcription factor network
nodes.analysis_detail: In the Vehicle neuronal TF regulatory network, hub regulators identified by out-degree centrality and integrator roles. Foxn1 identified as a broad cascade initiator and RXRalpha as a key integrator hub based on downstream target counts and network positioning.
nodes.supporting_quote: In contrast, the control network emphasized broad cascade initiators such as Foxn1 and key integrators like RXRalpha.

nodes.claim_text: RXR ChIP-seq identified approximately 12000 significant RXR-binding peaks in APP/PS1 mouse brain tissue.
nodes.experiment_detail: RXR ChIP-seq from APP/PS1 mouse brain tissue treated with Bexarotene (n=2) or Vehicle (n=2), using an anti-RXR antibody.
nodes.analysis_type: ChIP-seq peak calling
nodes.analysis_detail: Reads aligned to GRCm38 using Bowtie2 (v2.5.4). Low-quality reads (mapping score <2) removed by Samtools (v1.21). Peak calling performed with MACS2 at q-value threshold 0.05, comparing Bexarotene vs Vehicle. Total: 11,957 significant RXR-binding peaks.
nodes.supporting_quote: Peak calling identified 11,957 significant RXR-binding sites in RXR-activated versus Vehicle samples (Figure 5B).

nodes.claim_text: RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding at cis-regulatory elements was significantly altered by RXR activation.
nodes.experiment_detail: snATAC-seq and RXR ChIP-seq data from APP/PS1 mouse brains (Bexarotene vs Vehicle).
nodes.analysis_type: co-binding analysis at cis-regulatory elements
nodes.analysis_detail: Overlapping ATAC-seq/ChIP-seq peaks analyzed for RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding. Binding at shared sites compared between Bexarotene and Vehicle conditions (Supplementary Table S4).
nodes.supporting_quote: In our data, RXR/Nfatc2 co-binding was significantly altered by RXR activation (Table S4), underscoring its responsiveness to retinoid signaling

nodes.claim_text: RXR activation prominently affected transcription of lipid and sterol metabolism genes, including upregulation of Msmo1, Insig1, Hmgcr, Hmgcs1, Cyp51, and Idi1, as well as genes involved in lipid transport such as Lrp1 and Apod.
nodes.experiment_detail: scRNA-seq from APP/PS1 mouse brains treated with Bexarotene (n=4) or Vehicle (n=4), 37,634 cells after quality filtering.
nodes.analysis_type: differential gene expression analysis of lipid and sterol metabolism genes
nodes.analysis_detail: Differential expression between Bexarotene and Vehicle groups assessed using MAST with Bonferroni correction (p < 0.05). Genes in lipid and sterol metabolism pathways (Msmo1, Insig1, Hmgcr, Hmgcs1, Cyp51, Idi1) and lipid transport (Lrp1, Apod) identified among differentially expressed genes.
nodes.supporting_quote: the transcriptional programs we observed following Bexarotene treatment prominently affected lipid and sterol metabolism--such as upregulation of Msmo1, Insig1, Hmgcr, Hmgcs1, Cyp51, and Idi1--as well as genes involved in lipid transport and homeostasis (Lrp1, Apod)

nodes.claim_text: Nfatc2 physically interacts with RXRalpha and RARalpha, enhancing retinoic acid response element binding (Ohoka et al. 2011; Horsley and Pavlath 2002).
nodes.evidence_location: Section 3.7; citing Ohoka et al. 2011, Horsley and Pavlath 2002
nodes.supporting_quote: Nfatc2, a key immune regulator, has been shown to physically interact with RXRalpha and RARalpha, enhancing retinoic acid response element binding.

nodes.claim_text: Apoe is a known RXR target gene regulated through LXR/RXR heterodimer control, establishing its central role in RXR-mediated lipid regulation (established literature).
nodes.supporting_quote: Apoe is a known RXR target gene under LXR/RXR heterodimer control, highlighting the central role of RXR signaling in lipid regulation; its mRNA was significantly increased after Bexarotene treatment (log2FC = 0.42; adj-p = 3.23 x 10^-6).

excluded_borderline_claims.claim_text: RXR/LXR dimerization dominates the control regulatory state in APP/PS1 mouse brain, suggesting baseline RXR activity is driven by endogenous ligands.
excluded_borderline_claims.reason: Interpretive statement about the Vehicle baseline from the Discussion rather than a discrete experimental finding; the underlying footprinting data is captured through existing TF binding claims.

excluded_borderline_claims.claim_text: Bexarotene may enhance metabolic support, modulate glial activation programs, and improve resilience to inflammatory or injury-related stress in AD-like pathology.
excluded_borderline_claims.reason: Speculative inference about disease relevance from the Discussion without direct experimental support in this study.

excluded_borderline_claims.claim_text: The multi-modal analytical framework is broadly applicable to other nuclear receptors and context-dependent transcriptional regulators.
excluded_borderline_claims.reason: Methodological generalizability claim rather than a biological finding; no experimental evidence presented for other nuclear receptors.
